result: Core 1.6 code exposes JobDef ClearSnow with JobDriver_ClearSnowAndSand and Verse.SandGrid. Decompiler/ILSpy evidence confirms Map has public sandGrid, Map.ConstructComponents calls new SandGrid(this), Map.ExposeComponents recreates it only when ModsConfig.OdysseyActive during post-load, SandGrid constructor allocates its depth grid only after ModLister.CheckOdyssey("sand"), and JobDriver_ClearSnowAndSand clears snowGrid plus nullable sandGrid. Local official Data folder contains only Managed/Resources and no DLC data directories. Runtime on active load order Harmony/Core/HAR/CE/VF/ZLTestVehicle/ZLTestSupport/ZLTestAlienRace/Zombieland/RimBridgeServer loaded ZL_CE_EMPTY_00 visual-ready and warning-or-higher logs were empty. The clear-snow contract returned success=true skipped=true for mode sand with sandGridAvailable=false; current source also exposes odysseyInstalled, odysseyActive, sandGridPresent, and sandGridAvailable on that skip; the snow control returned success=true with snowBefore=1, snowAfter=0, worker contamination 0 -> 0.0443520024, and clean-filth subprobe success=true. Warning-or-higher logs stayed empty after both runs.
limitations: No save was written. This does not prove sand-clearing contamination transfer; it proves the current local install/load order cannot supply the Odyssey SandGrid runtime branch despite Core-visible code/types. Rerun with active Odyssey rather than instantiating a fake grid in the save.
notes: The bridge contract now reports missing Odyssey SandGrid support as a structured skip instead of throwing, and preserves the existing snow and clean-filth behavior.

The source-setting handoff targets still call FilthMaker in the expected place: CompSpawnerFilth.TrySpawnFilth after reachable-cell selection, Thing.TakeDamage through GenLeaving.DropFilthDueToDamage when SpawnFilth is true, HediffComp_DissolveGearOnDeath.Notify_PawnKilled for Props.filth, Projectile_Liquid.DoImpact before terrain/damage handling, DamageWorker_Flame.Apply after base damage when a non-pawn victim is destroyed, RoofCollapserImmediate.DropRoofInCellPhaseTwo for roofDef.filthLeaving, PregnancyUtility.SpawnBirthFilth inside its birth-filth loop, and Corpse.ButcherProducts after InnerPawn.ButcherProducts before butcher blood.

result: RimWorld 1.6 targeting and danger hooks still own the same decision boundaries used by Zombieland. BestAttackTarget keeps the full IAttackTargetSearcher signature and wraps a validator before selecting targets. GetAvailableShootingTargetsByScore still filters rawTargets through CanShootAtFromCurrentPosition and GetShootingTargetScore before returning weighted pairs. GenHostility.HostileTo keeps the Thing/Thing and Thing/Faction overloads used for zombie hostility overrides. JobDriver_Wait.CheckForAutoAttack still calls GenHostility.IsActiveThreatTo before adjacent melee auto-attack. ShotReport.AimOnTargetChance_StandardTarget still multiplies shooter, equipment, weather, gas, cover and execution factors. DangerWatcher.AffectsStoryDanger still delegates to GenHostility.IsActiveThreatToPlayer after defend/dormant gates. FleeUtility.ShouldFleeFrom still checks distance, alwaysFlee, hostility, ThreatDisabled/searcher status and optional LOS. Verb_MeleeAttack.TryCastShot still owns the immediate melee hit/dodge/damage path. Pawn_MeleeVerbs.GetUpdatedAvailableVerbsList still rebuilds melee verbs from pawn, equipment, apparel, hediff, mutant and terrain sources.
limitations: No runtime rerun was needed because current hostility/runtime contracts already prove player/enemy/animal/turret/mech target filtering, direct HostileTo overrides, drafted Wait_Combat auto-attack, tar-smoke aim suppression, danger/flee filtering, smart melee exits, and special bite/scratch melee verb filtering with warning-or-higher logs empty.
notes: Strengthens the hostility targeting and melee gate slice without broadening runtime coverage; future work should focus on long-form mixed-colony behavior only if it becomes a scenario row.

result: RimWorld 1.6 special-combat hooks still match their intended boundaries. DamageWorker_AddInjury.ApplyDamageToPart still resolves hit parts, calls ArmorUtility.GetPostArmorDamage, applies external-violence factors, and finalizes or propagates injury. ArmorUtility.GetPostArmorDamage still processes apparel armor then pawn armor and returns remaining damage. Verb_MeleeAttackDamage.DamageInfosToApply still yields melee DamageInfo values from adjusted melee damage, armor penetration, damage def/tool/source, then extra melee damages. Verb.CausesTimeSlowdown still gates slowdown on verb properties, pawn/turret targets, downed state and player/hostile factions. Pawn.TryGetAttackVerb still checks primary equipment, optional apparel/turrets, then melee fallback. Pawn.TryStartAttack still blocks busy/violent-disabled pawns before selecting a verb and starting the cast. StunHandler.Notify_DamageApplied still computes stun/adaptation and EMP stun before StunFor. Pawn_GeneTracker.AddGene keeps both public GeneDef and private Gene overloads, and Pawn_StoryTracker.SkinColorBase still lazily derives melanin or random skin-color genes before returning skinColorBase.
limitations: No runtime rerun was needed because current special-gauntlet and focused contracts already prove special damage scaling, tanky armor/shield/suit absorption, active electrifier melee side effects, attack-verb fallback/exclusion, far-zombie slowdown suppression, zombie violent-work-tag attack bypass, EMP disable, zombie gene rejection, and forced white zombie story color with warning-or-higher logs empty.
notes: Strengthens the special-damage and attack-verb slice while preserving existing visual/runtime ownership; visual appearance remains covered by F.RENDERING.VISUALS screenshots and render-node rows.

PawnRenderNode_Body.GraphicFor still resolves 1.6 body graphics through dessicated, mutant, creepjoiner and bodyType paths, while PawnRenderNode_Head.GraphicFor still returns null for no-head, skull graphics for dessicated pawns, then story head graphics. result: Decompiler confirmed RimWorld 1.6 JobDriver_ClearPollution.MakeNewToils still fails on unpolluted/unmarked cells, accumulates 5600 labor through tickIntervalAction, calls ClearPollutionAt, then spawns ThingDefOf.Wastepack; ClearPollutionAt still calls IntVec3.Unpollute and GridsUtility.Unpollute still clears PollutionGrid while adding only Odyssey sand mesh dirtying versus 1.4. A local fixture mod local.zltestpollution supplied DefOf names Wastepack and ClearPollution before Zombieland. Active load order Harmony/Core/HAR/CE/VF/ZLTestVehicle/ZLTestSupport/ZLTestAlienRace/ZLTestPollution/Zombieland/RimBridgeServer had no configuration/order/dependency/session/version issues. The improved clear-pollution bridge contract loaded ZL_CE_EMPTY_00 and returned success=true skipped=true with wastepackDefAvailable=true, clearPollutionJobDefAvailable=true, biotechActive=false, and reason that RimWorld pollution writes are still gated behind active Biotech. Warning-or-higher logs were empty before load, after startup, and after the contract.
limitations: This is not runtime proof of pollution transfer. It proves fixture XML alone is insufficient because PollutionGrid.SetPolluted calls the Biotech gate before mutating cells. Full runtime still requires active Biotech or an equivalent configuration that makes ModsConfig.BiotechActive true without faking the official expansion.
notes: The bridge contract now reports this hard gate cleanly instead of throwing on missing Area_PollutionClear or logging a Biotech-specific Set pollution error.

result: RimWorld 1.4 and 1.6 PawnDiedOrDownedThoughtsUtility.TryGiveThoughts keep the same public signature, and both versions route killed-child memory creation through AppendThoughts_ForHumanlike only when ModsConfig.BiotechActive is true, thoughtsKind is Died, the victim is non-adult, and the instigator pawn is adult. Runtime coverage still requires active Biotech or a legitimate Biotech-capable setup.
notes: This prevents retesting the adult death-thought suppression path and records the exact hard gate for the remaining child/killed-child branch.

result: Decompiler confirmed RimWorld 1.4 and 1.6 TunnelJellySpawner.Spawn(Map, IntVec3) share the same signature/body and still split jellyCount into ThingDefOf.InsectJelly stacks before calling GenSpawn.Spawn. notes: Warning-or-higher logs were empty before the rerun and after the successful contract. The bridge change extends an existing reusable C3 contract rather than adding a one-off endpoint.

Source sweep found the remaining DLC-facing mod surfaces are Biotech-linked pollution, mech repair/disassembly, gene/subcore products, child killed-thought branches, child zombie body/visual support, gene rejection, mech targeting, and MayRequire ToxicEnvironmentResistance XML stats. Decompiler confirmed PollutionGrid.SetPolluted gates writes behind ModLister.CheckBiotech("Set pollution"), JobDriver_RepairMech.MakeNewToils and JobDriver_DisassembleMech.MakeNewToils exit behind ModLister.CheckBiotech, Building_SubcoreScanner.Tick, Building_GeneExtractor.Finish and Building_GeneAssembler.Finish still make/place their products through the expected ThingMaker/GenPlace sites, PawnDiedOrDownedThoughtsUtility.AppendThoughts_ForHumanlike still adds KilledChild only when ModsConfig.BiotechActive and child/adult conditions hold, CompPollutionPump.Pump makes Wastepack after unpolluting, CompWasteProducer.ProduceWaste makes Wastepack stacks into an inner holder, and CompMechCarrier.PostSpawnSetup no longer calls ThingMaker.MakeThing.

result: Source comparison showed ZombieWeather.cs is unchanged since the port baseline, so this pass rechecked the unchanged threat/weather code against current RimWorld 1.6 touchpoints rather than assuming old evidence still applies. RimWorld 1.6 still exposes GlobalControlsUtility.DoDate(float,float,ref float) as a compact DateReadout wrapper that decrements curBaseY by DateReadout.Height; DateReadout.Height remains 48 plus optional season-label height. DateReadout.DateOnGUI still handles the vanilla hover tooltip after drawing the right-aligned date group, so Zombieland's separate right-aligned zombie-count and dynamic-threat readouts remain semantically isolated below the vanilla date readout. IncidentWorker.TryExecute still calls TryExecuteWorker before tale/codex side effects and returns that flag. PawnGroupKindWorker.GeneratePawns still returns the generated pawn list after the worker fills it. IncidentWorker_Raid.TryExecuteWorker still generates raid pawns, sends a standard letter, and makes lords after TryGenerateRaidInfo. Zombieland's threat factor is still consumed by TickManager.ZombieTicking, TickManager.CanHaveMoreZombies, ZombieIncidents.ZombiesForNewIncident, and the incident pawn infection gate.
limitations: Static evidence only; runtime incident, forecast UI, zero-threat and raid-routing behavior remain claimed by the existing S-Incident-Threat scenario rather than this row.
notes: Prevents unchanged baseline files from being skipped silently during coverage work and confirms no new local runtime pass is justified for the threat/weather spine unless ZombieWeather, date UI, incident execution, pawn group generation, or raid execution changes.

result: Source comparison showed PheromoneGrid.cs is byte-identical to the a0de6c309ab789a488a6bc106d281b5d0ca79020 port baseline, so this pass rechecked the unchanged grid against current RimWorld 1.6 map lifecycle and current Zombieland consumers. RimWorld 1.6 Map.ExposeData still calls ConstructComponents during LoadingVars, then ExposeComponents; ExposeComponents still deep-scribes the components list and calls FillComponents; FillComponents still instantiates missing non-abstract MapComponent subclasses; Map.FinalizeLoading still spawns loaded things and then calls FinalizeInit. PheromoneGrid remains a normal MapComponent with deep-scribed Pheromone[] cells, timestamp/zombieCount fields, legacy square-map fallback for missing mapx/mapz, and bounds-checked GetPheromone reads/writes. Tools.GetGrid still caches by Map, falls back to map.GetComponent<PheromoneGrid>(), and adds a new component only if absent. Zombieland resets transient zombieCount in both TickManager.FinalizeInit and Map.FinalizeLoading_Patch, while runtime evidence already proves first movement after load repopulates destination counts and that Thing.Position, projectile, CE projectile, vehicle, thumper, and senses paths write/read timestamps through the same grid.
limitations: Static evidence only; it does not replace the existing live pheromone/senses, position-side-effect, dense horde, projectile, CE, vehicle, thumper, or save-load runtime evidence. No new runtime contract was justified because this pass found no RimWorld 1.6 lifecycle drift or stale PheromoneGrid target.
notes: Prevents unchanged PheromoneGrid.cs from being skipped silently during coverage work and records why future reruns should be triggered by PheromoneGrid serialization, Map component lifecycle, grid count reset hooks, or timestamp writer changes rather than by baseline uncertainty.

result: Source and asset comparison showed ZombieStains.cs, VariableGraphic.cs, GraphicsDatabase.cs, TextureAtlas.cs, PartsNew atlas files, and SkinColors.png are unchanged from the port baseline. RimWorld 1.6 Graphic.Init still has signature Init(GraphicRequest req), GraphicDatabase.GetInner<T>(GraphicRequest) still instantiates T, calls value.Init(req), and caches by GraphicRequest, and GraphicDatabase.Get(Type,string,Shader,Vector2,Color,Color,GraphicData,List<ShaderParameter>,string) still funnels through a GraphicRequest with the same graphicClass/path/shader/drawSize/color/colorTwo/data/shaderParameters/maskPath shape. The current atlas metadata still contains all ten stain paths referenced by ZombieStains, and VariableGraphic still uses body/head stain tables through GraphicsDatabase.GetColorData plus VariableMaterial. Existing startup evidence already initialized Zombieland graphics without config errors, and visual-gauntlet evidence shows generated zombie visuals before and after save-load.
limitations: Static evidence only; it does not prove a pixel-by-pixel stain distribution or replace the existing visual screenshots. No new runtime screenshot pass was justified because this pass found no RimWorld 1.6 GraphicRequest/init drift, missing atlas paths, or changed visual asset inputs.
notes: Records the unchanged generated-stain/variable-graphic surface so future reruns are triggered by changed stain tables, atlas inputs, GraphicRequest/GraphicDatabase lifecycle, VariableGraphic/VariableMaterial code, or reported visual regressions rather than by baseline uncertainty.

result: Initial comparison showed JobGiver.cs, Zombie_ThinkTree.xml and Zombie_Jobs.xml were unchanged from the port baseline. RimWorld 1.6 ThinkNode_Priority.TryIssueJobPackage still iterates subNodes in XML order and returns the first valid ThinkResult, while ThinkNode_JobGiver.TryIssueJobPackage still calls TryGiveJob, returns NoJob for null, applies optional report-string overrides, and returns ThinkResult(job,this). Current Zombie_ThinkTree.xml binds only JobGiver_Stumble, JobGiver_Sabotage, JobGiver_Blob and JobGiver_Spitter; Zombie_Race.xml assigns normal, blob and spitter races to those think trees; Zombie_Jobs.xml binds those job defs to JobDriver_Stumble, JobDriver_Sabotage, JobDriver_Blob and JobDriver_Spitter. Source and installed-mod scans found no references to JobGiver_ExtractZombieSerum or JobGiver_DoubleTap outside their definitions, and current serum extraction/double-tap behavior is owned by WorkGiver_ExtractZombieSerum and WorkGiver_DoubleTap through Zombie_Workgivers.xml plus runtime settings/infection evidence. The unused legacy ThinkNode classes were removed from JobGiver.cs.
limitations: Static/source cleanup evidence only; it does not replace live Stumble/Sabotage/Blob/Spitter runtime evidence or the existing WorkGiver-based serum/double-tap runtime evidence. No live RimWorld pass was justified because the only code change removes unreachable classes and the active XML/job-driver bindings are unchanged.
notes: Prevents future retesting of unbound legacy JobGiver_ExtractZombieSerum/JobGiver_DoubleTap classes; rerun if Zombie_ThinkTree.xml, Zombie_Jobs.xml, Zombie_Workgivers.xml, JobGiver.cs, or RimWorld ThinkNode_JobGiver dispatch changes.

result: Shocker and switchable helper sources were reread against current RimWorld 1.6 surfaces. ThingComp.PostExposeData, CompGetGizmosExtra and CompTick still match the helper overrides; Command.ProcessInput and GizmoOnGUI match Command_SimpleToggle; InheritInteractionsFrom is inherited from Gizmo and Command_Toggle still exposes the isActive delegate shape used for interaction inheritance; PlaceWorker.DrawGhost and AllowsPlacing match PlaceWorker_ZombieShocker; Effecter.Trigger still dispatches the target pair used by ZombieShocker.ReceiveCompSignal; and SubEffecter.SubTrigger/SubEffectTick remain virtual with the 1.6 force parameter already present in SubEffecter_ZombieShocker. Source search confirmed current player activation flows through the float-menu ZapZombies job into ZombieShocker.ReceiveCompSignal("Activate"), while CompActivatable.Activate has no active caller and its old gizmo path is commented.
limitations: Static evidence only; runtime behavior remains owned by the existing S-Defense-Room save-load, zap, placement, gizmo, visual and sound evidence. This pass intentionally does not remove CompActivatable because the no-data comp remains XML-bound on ZombieShocker and removal could change saved def/component shape without improving 1.6 behavior.
notes: Closes the unchanged helper-source ambiguity for shocker and thumper support without adding a one-off bridge endpoint; rerun if the helper classes, ZombieShocker XML comps/effecter binding, ZapZombies job flow, or RimWorld ThingComp/Command/PlaceWorker/SubEffecter signatures change.

result: Small supporting helpers were reread as backstops rather than new behavior rows. BrainzThought is the only changed file in this set since the port baseline: it now returns CurStageIndex 0 instead of throwing NotImplementedException, while RimWorld 1.6 Thought still exposes abstract CurStageIndex plus virtual ExposeData and MoodOffset. CountingCache is a local generic cache with one current consumer, ZombieAreaManager pawn-head textures for dangerous-area overlays. Dialog_ErrorMessage still overrides Window.InitialSize and DoWindowContents; RimWorld 1.6 only adds Window drawing infrastructure and keeps the scroll/label widget signatures used by the dialog. DisposableMaterial remains local cleanup for VariableMaterial-created main textures, and MaterialPool still exposes the MaterialRequest overload used by visual code. VictimHead uses the current PawnCacheRenderer.RenderPawn overload where 1.6 removed the old renderBody parameter; the current six-argument call relies on the remaining defaulted 1.6 parameters and MaterialPool.MatFrom(MaterialRequest) still exists.
limitations: Static evidence only. Player-facing behavior remains owned by startup_support_state normal-path errorCount=0, dangerous-area overlay visual evidence, variable-graphics visual scenarios, and the chainsaw slaughter/victim-head runtime evidence. No new runtime contract was justified because no unsupported hook, stale call shape, or independent player workflow was found.
notes: Prevents small helper files from being treated as untested standalone features; rerun only if these helpers or their owner flows change, or if a reported issue names startup error dialogs, dangerous-area head texture caching, generated material cleanup, BRRAINZ thought bubbles, or chainsaw victim-head rendering.

result: Infection/medical source was reread against current RimWorld 1.6 surfaces. The direct hediff overrides used by ZombieBite and ZombieInfection still exist on Verse.Hediff: ExposeData, PostAdd, Tick, Heal, TryMergeWith, LabelInBrackets, LabelColor, PainFactor, PainOffset, SummaryHealthPercentImpact, BleedRate and ShouldRemove. HediffComp_Zombie_Infecter/TendDuration still target valid HediffComp hooks: CompExposeData, CompPostPostAdd, CompTended(float,float,int), CompShouldRemove, CompTipStringExtra and CompStateIcon. Recipe_CureZombieInfection overrides stable Verse.RecipeWorker GetPartsToApplyOn, ApplyOnPawn and GetLabelWhenUsedOn signatures. The extract/double-tap/rope jobs still override stable JobDriver reservation, toils, ExposeData and report surfaces, while SpecialThingFilterWorker.Matches remains the correct serum filter hook. 1.4-to-1.6 comparison found Hediff/HediffComp and JobDriver added tick-interval and notification surfaces, but the signatures used here remain valid. The only changed files in this support set since the port baseline are already-covered port adaptations: DoubleTap moved from OnCell to ClosestTouch and WorkGivers switched to PathFinder.FindPathNow plus null-faction chainsaw repair acceptance.
limitations: Static evidence only; runtime behavior remains owned by infection_medical_state, infection_workflow_state, zombie_extract_filter_visibility, convert_infected_corpse_to_zombie, zombie_corpse_alert_forbid_contract, and settings/workgiver evidence. No new live pass was justified because this pass found no stale override, missing hook, or unsupported source path beyond variants already marked additive.
notes: Closes the source/decompiler backstop for infection hediffs, cure recipe, extract/double-tap/rope jobs, serum filter, infection alerts and infection workgiver support; rerun if these classes, their XML bindings, RimWorld Hediff/HediffComp/RecipeWorker/JobDriver/SpecialThingFilterWorker surfaces, or the named infection runtime fixtures change.
